q.en: Who signed the bill that created Yellowstone, the world's first national park, in 1872?
q.zh: 谁在1872年签署了创建世界上第一个国家公园——黄石公园的法案？

options.en: President Theodore Roosevelt according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 西奥多·罗斯福总统（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states: "President Ulysses S. Grant signed the bill creating Yellowstone National Park in Wyoming, Idaho, and Montana" in 1872.
explain.zh: 书中写道：1872年“尤利西斯·S·格兰特总统签署了创建黄石国家公园的法案”，公园位于怀俄明、爱达荷和蒙大拿。

q.en: According to the glossary, what does the word "migrate" mean?
q.zh: 根据词汇表，“migrate（迁徙）”是什么意思？

options.en: To move from one area to another every year
options.zh: 每年从一个地区移动到另一个地区

options.en: To mix fresh and salt water according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 混合淡水和咸水（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines "migrate" as "move from one area to another every year (p. 9)."
explain.zh: 词汇表将“migrate”定义为“每年从一个地区移动到另一个地区”（第9页）。

q.en: In the glossary, what is an "estuary"?

options.en: The end of a river where fresh water mixes with salt water
options.zh: 河流尽头淡水与咸水混合的地方

options.en: A narrow sea inlet between steep slopes according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 陡峭山坡之间的狭长海湾（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines "estuary" as "the end of a river where the fresh water mixes with salt water (p. 14)."
explain.zh: 词汇表将“estuary”定义为“河流尽头淡水与咸水混合的地方”（第14页）。

q.en: The Masai people call the Serengeti "Siringitu." What does that mean?
q.zh: 马赛人称塞伦盖蒂为“Siringitu”，这是什么意思？

options.en: "The place where the land goes on forever"
"Land of the wildebeest" according to some versions of the story
options.zh: “角马的领地”（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says: "To the Masai, the Serengeti has always been Siringitu: 'the place where the land goes on forever.'"
explain.zh: 书中说：对马赛人来说，塞伦盖蒂一直是 Siringitu，即“土地一直延伸到的地方”。

q.en: Which animal lives in the Saguenay-St. Lawrence park year-round?
q.zh: 哪种动物全年都生活在萨格奈-圣劳伦斯保护区内？

options.en: The Florida panther according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 佛罗里达黑豹（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The rare beluga, or white whale

explain.en: The book states: "The rare beluga, or white whale, lives here year-round."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“稀有的白鲸全年都生活在这里。”

q.en: According to the "Parks Under Siege" section, which of these is listed as a threat to national parks?
q.zh: 根据“受到威胁的公园”一节，以下哪项被列为国家公园面临的威胁？

options.en: Too many visitors leaving litter and trampling trails
options.zh: 过多游客留下垃圾、踩踏小径

options.en: A lack of mountains according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 缺少山脉（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book lists threats such as "too many visitors can cause plenty of problems, from leaving behind litter to trampling trails."
explain.zh: 书中列出的威胁包括“太多游客会造成很多问题，从留下垃圾到踩踏小径”。

q.en: In which country is Sareks National Park, where the Saami herd reindeer above the Arctic Circle?
q.zh: 萨雷克国家公园位于哪个国家？那里的萨米人在北极圈以北放牧驯鹿。

explain.en: The book says: "Sweden was the first country in Europe to create national parks. This one, far north above the Arctic Circle... is home to the Saami, or Lapp people, who herd reindeer."
explain.zh: 书中说：瑞典是欧洲第一个建立国家公园的国家，萨雷克位于北极圈以北，是萨米人放牧驯鹿的地方。

q.en: Tassili N'Ajjer National Park in Algeria preserves more than how many drawings and engravings?
q.zh: 阿尔及利亚的塔西利·恩·阿耶尔国家公园保留了多少幅以上的绘画和雕刻？

options.en: More than 50,000 according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 超过50,000幅（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states the landscape "left behind more than 15,000 drawings and engravings in caves and on the sides of cliffs."
explain.zh: 书中写道，该地貌在洞穴和悬崖上留下了“超过15,000幅绘画和雕刻”。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing "National Parks"?
q.zh: 作者写《国家公园》的主要目的什么？

options.en: To teach readers how to build a park according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 教读者如何建造公园（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To argue that parks should be closed
To inform readers about the value and variety of national parks around the world
options.zh: 向读者介绍世界各地国家公园的价值和多样性

options.en: To entertain with a fictional story
options.zh: 用一个虚构故事来娱乐

explain.en: The book is an informational text that explores many real parks and explains "The Value of Parks," showing the author aims to inform.
explain.zh: 这是一本科普读物，探讨了许多真实的公园并解释“公园的价值”，说明作者的目的是向读者传递信息。

q.en: Which park is in Hawaii and lets visitors watch the world's largest, most active volcano?
q.zh: 哪个公园位于夏威夷，让游客可以观看世界上最大、最活跃的火山？

explain.en: The book says of Volcanoes National Park, Hawaii: "In this park, you can view the world's largest, most active volcano."
explain.zh: 书中关于夏威夷火山国家公园写道：“在这个公园里，你可以看到世界上最大、最活跃的火山。”

prompt.en: Choose one national park from the book and draw a postcard showing its most famous feature (a geyser, a whale, a volcano, etc.), then write three sentences telling a visitor why they should go there.
prompt.zh: 从书中选择一个国家公园，画一张明信片，展示它最著名的特色（间歇泉、鲸鱼、火山等），然后写三句话告诉游客为什么应该去那里。

en: Pick a park such as Yellowstone, Saguenay-St. Lawrence, or Volcanoes and name its key feature.
Use the book's details (e.g., geysers, belugas, lava) to make your drawing and sentences accurate.
zh: 选择一个公园，如黄石、萨格奈-圣劳伦斯或火山公园，并说出它的主要特色。
利用书中的细节（如间歇泉、白鲸、熔岩）让你的图画和句子准确。
